enrichment.short_description: Centralized secrets management platform for storing, syncing, and rotating API keys and credentials across infrastructure, CI/CD, and AI agents.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Doppler is a secrets management platform for securely storing, syncing, and rotating API keys and credentials across your infrastructure.

features.title: Centralized secrets storage
features.description: Stores API keys, database credentials, and cloud access tokens in one place instead of scattering them across config files and scripts.

features.title: Dynamic secrets
features.description: Auto-rotating credentials that self-destruct after deployment, reducing the window a leaked secret stays valid.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Syncs secrets directly into Vercel, Heroku, AWS Secrets Manager, GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.

features.title: Audit logging
features.description: Logs all secret access for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance requirements.

features.title: AI agent access via MCP
features.description: Provides an MCP server and OIDC-scoped service accounts so AI agents can request secrets without custom scripts.

features.title: Change requests and secret rotation
features.description: Team plan adds approval workflows for secret changes plus automatic rotation.

features.title: SAML SSO and role-based access
features.description: Team plan includes SAML SSO and RBAC for controlling who can view or edit secrets.

pros: Free tier available for teams of up to 3 users
SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliant, with full access logging
Native integrations with major cloud, CI/CD, and deployment platforms
Built-in MCP support for scoping secret access to AI agents
cons: Per-user pricing ($8-$21/user/month) can grow costly for larger teams
Enterprise-only features like Enterprise Key Management and custom SLAs require quote-only pricing
Add-ons such as custom roles, user groups, and integration syncs cost extra per seat

faqs.question: Does Doppler have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Developer plan is free for up to 3 users, with additional users billed at $8/month each.

faqs.question: Does Doppler support AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, Doppler provides an MCP server and OIDC-scoped service accounts so AI agents can request secrets without embedding credentials in code.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Doppler have?
faqs.answer: Doppler is SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliant, with complete audit logging of secret access.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: The Team plan ($21/user/month) includes a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Can Doppler be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan supports on-premise or cloud deployment options.

use_cases.title: Centralizing secrets across environments
use_cases.description: Replace scattered .env files and config secrets with a single synced source across dev, staging, and production.

use_cases.title: Securing AI agent credential access
use_cases.description: Scope and audit the credentials individual AI agents can access using OIDC-based service accounts and MCP.

use_cases.title: Automating secret injection in CI/CD
use_cases.description: Sync secrets automatically into GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, and other pipeline tools to avoid hardcoding credentials.

features: SAML SSO and role-based access controls
Change requests and automatic secret rotation

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Doppler?
paragraphs: Doppler is a secrets management platform that centralizes API keys, database credentials, and cloud access tokens so teams stop copying them across config files and scripts.

It syncs secrets into tools like Vercel, Heroku, AWS Secrets Manager, GitHub Actions, and Kubernetes, and adds audit logging, dynamic (auto-rotating) secrets, and MCP-based access scoping for AI agents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Doppler is for
paragraphs: Doppler suits engineering teams that need a single source of truth for secrets across development, staging, and production, as well as organizations running AI agents that need scoped, auditable credential access rather than hardcoded keys.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source platform for managing application secrets, PKI certificates, and privileged access across cloud and AI infrastructure.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Infisical is an open-source secrets management, certificate (PKI), and privileged access platform for developers and AI agents.

features.title: Secrets management
features.description: Centralized storage, rotation, and auditing of application credentials with RBAC, approval workflows, and dynamic secret issuance.

features.title: Certificate management (PKI)
features.description: Automates private certificate authority operations including issuance, renewal, revocation, and discovery.

features.title: Privileged access management (PAM)
features.description: Grants short-lived, identity-based access sessions with session recording and AI-generated summaries.

features.title: Agent Vault
features.description: Gives AI agents access to credentials they need without exposing the underlying secret values directly to the agent.

features.title: Dynamic secrets
features.description: Issues short-lived, on-demand credentials scoped to individual requests instead of long-lived static secrets.

features.title: Broad integrations
features.description: Connects with AWS, Kubernetes, Docker, GitHub, GitLab, Terraform, Ansible, Jenkins, Vercel, Heroku, Azure, GCP, and Datadog.

pros: Free-forever plan covering up to 5 identities and 3 projects
Open source with a self-hosting option in addition to cloud
Combines secrets management, PKI, and privileged access management in one platform
Wide range of native integrations across cloud and CI/CD tooling
cons: Pro plan is priced per identity ($18/month), which scales with team/agent count
Advanced capabilities like dynamic secrets, AI security advisor, SCIM, and LDAP require the custom-priced Enterprise plan
Free tier is capped at 5 identities, 3 projects, 3 environments, and 10 integrations

faqs.question: Does Infisical have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan is free forever and includes up to 5 identities, 3 projects, and 3 environments.

faqs.question: Is Infisical open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Infisical is an open-source project that also offers self-hosting.

faqs.question: Does Infisical support certificate management?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes PKI features for automated certificate authority issuance, renewal, and revocation.

faqs.question: Does Infisical support AI agent credential access?
faqs.answer: Yes, through Agent Vault, which lets AI agents use credentials without exposing the secret values directly.

faqs.question: How is the Pro plan priced?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan costs $18 per identity per month and adds features like secret rotation, SAML SSO, and 90-day audit log retention.

use_cases.title: Centralizing application secrets
use_cases.description: Store, rotate, and audit credentials across environments with RBAC and approval workflows.

use_cases.title: Managing internal certificates
use_cases.description: Automate private CA issuance, renewal, and revocation instead of handling certificates manually.

use_cases.title: Securing AI agent access to credentials
use_cases.description: Use Agent Vault to let AI agents access the secrets they need without exposing raw credential values.

features: Higher rate limits and priority support
Dynamic secrets and AI security advisor
Audit log streaming and custom retention

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Infisical?
paragraphs: Infisical is an open-source platform that combines secrets management, certificate (PKI) management, and privileged access management so teams don't need separate tools for each.

It supports self-hosting or cloud deployment, integrates with tools like AWS, Kubernetes, GitHub, and Terraform, and includes an Agent Vault feature for giving AI agents scoped access to credentials without exposing the raw secret values.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Infisical is for
paragraphs: Infisical fits development and security teams that want centralized control over secrets and certificates, particularly those that prefer an open-source, self-hostable option or need to secure credential access for AI agents alongside human users.
